_comment: Evergreen SEO keyword backlog. The evening evergreen-seo-pipeline takes the top 'queued' entry each day (order = priority), drafts a pillar article for it, and marks it 'used' (with slug + usedAt). Volumes/KD from DataForSEO US/en, checked 2026-07-09. Founder can reorder/add/skip entries; set status 'skipped' to veto a topic. Replenish when queue runs low: DataForSEO keyword_suggestions (creds in .env), filter vol>=100, exclude local/navigational intent and competitor brand terms, dedup vs ledger.json topics.

cluster: will radiology be replaced by ai (720)
queue.intent: informational — the classic question, huge combined volume, near-zero difficulty
queue.angle: Honest, data-driven answer: what AI actually does today (drafting, triage), what it can't (accountability, signature), what the workforce data shows. Natural home for the draft-then-sign narrative.

queue.intent: informational pillar — definitive 'what is teleradiology' guide
queue.angle: How teleradiology works in 2026: models (prelim vs final), nighthawk history, credentialing/licensing, economics, where AI drafting fits. Must NOT cannibalize /ai-vs-teleradiology (comparison intent) or /for-teleradiology-companies (landing) — this is the neutral educational pillar that links to both.

Complete guide to radiology reporting: narrative vs structured, ACR/RSNA templates, turnaround benchmarks, dictation → AI drafting evolution, what ready-to-sign means. Links to ct-report-turnaround-time.

queue.intent: informational — after-hours coverage, directly adjacent to xAID's value prop
queue.angle: What nighthawk radiology is, its history (NightHawk Radiology Services), overnight preliminary-read economics, and how AI drafting changes after-hours coverage math.

The radiologist shortage in numbers, 2026 edition: AAMC projections, residency pipeline, retirement wave, imaging volume growth. Internal-link hub for salary-transparency, best-metro, overutilization articles.

queue.intent: career — audience-building like the salary-transparency article (worked before)
queue.angle: Teleradiology careers guide: how remote reading works, comp vs on-site, licensing across states, what groups look for. Radiologist-audience authority play; links salary-transparency and best-metro.

queue.keyword: how to choose a teleradiology company
queue.intent: commercial — buyer checklist, very high CPC = real purchase intent
queue.angle: Buyer's guide: prelim vs final reads, turnaround SLAs, subspecialty coverage, QA process, pricing models, questions to ask. Neutral checklist tone; careful not to cannibalize /for-teleradiology-companies (that page sells, this one educates the buyer).

queue.intent: informational pillar — biggest prize, hardest; do LAST when the cluster has internal links to feed it
queue.angle: The complete 2026 guide to AI in radiology: detection vs drafting vs triage, FDA/CE landscape, evidence base, workflow integration. Check cannibalization vs ai-radiology-glossary and foundation-models articles before drafting — this should be the hub they all link to.
